definition: average level of the sea surface over a relatively short period (usually in the range of 10-30 minutes), being  used to distinguish water level variations (surges, tides) from shorter-period sea surface waves

definition_ml.en: average level of the sea surface over a relatively short period (usually in the range of 10-30 minutes), being  used to distinguish water level variations (surges, tides) from shorter-period sea surface waves

source_document: Federal Maritime and Hydrographical Agency, Germany
note: As with the water current, one can distinguish between tidal and non-tidal “surge” components of still water level.

note_ml.en: As with the water current, one can distinguish between tidal and non-tidal “surge” components of still water level.
